text.title: python Output Contract
text.summary: Required project layout and config invariants.

section.title: JSON Schema

example.code:
# pyproject.toml [tool.poetry] python = "^3.11"
[tool.poetry.dependencies] django = "^5.0"
[tool.ruff] target-version = "py311"
[tool.ruff.lint] select = ["E", "F", "I", "B", "C4", "UP", "SIM", "DJ"]
[tool.mypy] strict = true python_version = "3.11"
# project layout # myapp/ # ├── services.py # business logic # ├── views.py # HTTP/transport only # ├── models.py # └── tests/ # └── test_services.py

# REJECTED from typing import Optional, List # r6 violated
def create_user(request) -> Optional[User]: # logic in views, Optional[X], request param if not request.POST.get("email"): return None return User.objects.create(email=request.POST["email"]) # no @transaction.atomic
example.note: 4 violations: Optional[X] / List (r6), logic in view (r1), request param (r1), no transaction.atomic (r1).

list.item: ruff configured; no black / isort / flake8 in dev deps.
services/ layer exists; views import services, not models directly.
`X | None` syntax used; no `Optional[X]` in new code.
asyncio code uses gather() or TaskGroup() for concurrent I/O; no sequential `await` for independent ops.
